title: rosa viscaria subsp. rubiginosa
intro: Rosa viscaria subsp. rubiginosa, also known as the Alpine Rose or Alpine Sweetbriar, is a subspecies of the Rosa viscaria plant, a type of flowering shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Rosa viscaria subsp. rubiginosa include its potential to soothe and calm skin irritations, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical applications like salves and creams, to harness its medicinal properties and therapeutic benefits.
